title: Random Accessories
description: Random Accessories is a Minecraft Mod that lets you equip quality of life accessories into your accessories equipment tab!
body:
## **![](https://i.imgur.com/DT2IPbs.png)**

 

## **Intro:**

*   Random Accessories is a Minecraft Mod that lets you equip quality of life accessories into your accessories equipment tab! This mod is targeting Minecraft 1.21.8 on Fabric.
*   This mod currently contains 9 accessories that bring quality of life to Minecraft. More are planned in the future.

## **Included Accessories:**
*   __Aegis Charm__ (requires both Sentinel Charm + Vanguard Charm - Overall damage reduction)
*   __Builders Glove__ (extends the block placement range)
*   __Burst Shoes__ (dash in any direction - both double tap and keybind)
*   __Foreman's Helmet__ (faster mining)
*   __Gravity Dampener__ (reduced fall damage, up to no fall damage)
*   __Magnetic Glove__ (suck up mined blocks/items from 8 blocks away or higher)
*   __Mercury Anklet__ (faster movement)
*   __Poseidon's Crown__ (nightvision underwater, higher damage underwater, better underwater mining)
*   __Replica Phoenix Cape__ (Save your inventory when you die …. once)
*   __Ring of Vulcanis__ (Reduced Fire damage - Lava, fire, blazes, ghasts)
*   __Scuba Flippers__ (faster swimming)
*   __Sentinel Charm__ (Ranged Damage Reduction)
*   __Vanguard Charm__ (Melee Damage Reduction)
*   __Ultra Jump Belt__ (Double Jump, Ledge Climb Saving Grace)

## **Dependencies:**

*   Random Accessories requires the Accessories Mod. That mod allows us to create accessories for Minecraft and to socket them into the secondary equipment tab.

## **Notes:**

*   I may change some of the recipes in the future. Not sure if I like some of the recipes quite yet.

## **Frequently Asked Questions:**

*   Q: How do I change the config?
*   A: In the config, there is a json file. OR, directly through mod menu!
*   Q: Can you add this cool accessory idea to the mod?
*   A: If it's possible and if I have the time, I'll add it!
*   Q: Can this mod be used in modpacks?
*   A: Of course!
